review_summary: The transcript is sufficient for application-oriented questions on visually guided audio source separation and why efficient video understanding remains an important systems problem.

question_bank.question: What is visually guided audio source separation?
choices: Using visual information from the video to help separate a mixed audio signal into its component sources
Using audio only to predict object bounding boxes
Training a model to remove all sound from a video
Compressing sound waves into fewer frequency bins
question_bank.explanation: In a mixed recording, the visual stream can reveal which objects or speakers are active. That information can guide the system to separate the combined audio into meaningful parts.

question_bank.question: Why is efficient video understanding an important research problem?
choices: Long videos are too expensive to process exhaustively clip by clip, so models must decide what to analyze
Video models have already solved every recognition task
Audio makes video shorter and easier to store
Efficient methods always avoid using learned features
question_bank.explanation: A long video can contain many clips, and processing all of them can be prohibitively expensive. Efficient methods try to focus computation on important moments or useful modalities.

transcript_quotes: cannot do clip one by one
